Primary Needs for Home Health (check all that apply):
When we call, we will also need to obtain the following information before seeing the
patient. If you have these available, feel free to fax along with this form.
Recent H&P or recent progress notes
HPI: This is an 80 year old female who:
1. is being seen for a chief complaint of L KNEE. PT COMES IN TODAY WITH LEFT KNEE PAIN. STATES IT HAS
BEEN ONGOING FOR A WHILE. SHE HAD MRI DONE AT DES RAD, AND X RAYS DONE IN THE OFFICE.
Examination today reveals crepitus and pain throughout range of motion left knee. Mild valgus deformity. Calf is soft
and nontender. Stable ligamentous examination. Well-healed arthroscopic portals
Patient Specific Notes: The patient has osteoarthritis left knee. She has been treated conservatively as well as
with an arthroscopic debridement. She says she is ready for a total knee replacement. She did have an MRI
scan of the left knee which shows lateral meniscus pathology as well as lateral compartment arthritis. She will
need preoperative medical clearance. Follow-up for final surgical decision making visit
History of arthroscopy of knee joint
Musculoskeletal care: I advised the patient that most patients with degenerative joint disease of the knee can
achieve some measure of pain relief from nonsurgical options like medications, lifestyle modification, exercise and
physical therapy, and use of supportive devices or change in footwear. The importance of maintaining an ideal
body weight or losing weight to reduce stress on the knee joint is a cornestone of nonoperative management.
Expectations: I counseled the patient about the natural history of degenerative joint disease of the knee which
typically has exacerbations and remissions. Many people report that changes in the weather also affect the
degree of pain from arthritis. The knee joint does not heal on its own when there is arthritis. With time, the
symptoms of arthritis may get worse. For some, conservative management may not be not satisfactory. These
Contact Office if your knee pain worsens, or if the knee becomes warm or accompanied by a fever.
Follow up. Other Instructions: AFTER SURGICAL DECISION MAKING
Electronically Signed By: Gary Morris, 03/05/2026 12:01 PM PST
